Description: Unique identifier for each accident event.
Other information: Serves as the primary key used to join tables together.

Description: Light condition at the time of the accident.
Other information:
Represents the light condition at the time and location of the accident. The categories are: 
- 1 (Day) 
- 2 (Dusk/dawn)
- 3 (Dark street lights on) 
- 4 (Dark street lights off)
- 5 (Dark no street lights)
- 6 (Dark street lights unknown)
- 9 (Unknown)

Description: Speed limit of the location where the accident occurred.
Other information: Represents the speed limit at the location of the accident. The unit is km/h.

Description: Sex of the drivers involved in the accidents.
Other information:
Represents the sex of the drivers. The categories are:
- M (Male)
- F (Female)

Description: Age group of the drivers involved in the accidents.
Other information:
Represents the recoded age groups of the drivers. The categories are:
- 0-15 
- 16-21 
- 22-29 
- 30-49 
- 50-59 
- 60-69 
- 70+
The unit is years.

Description: Name of the local government area where the accident occurred.
Other information: Represents the LGA where the accident occurred.

Description: Month in which the accident occurred.
Other information: This variable was transformed by extracting the month information from the original variable ACCIDENT_DATE, which records the day, month, and year when the accident occurred. It is stored as a factor ranging from Jan to Dec.

Description: Year in which the accident occurred.
Other information: This variable was transformed by extracting the year information from the original variable ACCIDENT_DATE, which records the day, month, and year when the accident occurred. It is stored as a numeric variable.
